title: New strategy idea-sourcing memo (selection/research only) using completed validation-factory lessons

provenance.head_note: HEAD is exactly the Factory-D11 commit 42b5db7; factory tree clean, nothing staged, no automation-touched factory files since D11.

1_current_factory_complete_context.summary: The reusable research-validation factory (Factory-D1 through D11) is complete, committed, and demonstrated end-to-end on synthetic data. It is NOT a paper/live system.
available_modules: validation_decision (research-decision + readiness gate; paper/live unreachable by default)
1_current_factory_complete_context.implication: Any new frozen S29 hypothesis can be run through the same controlled, anti-overfit ladder with uniform reports and identical git/data guardrails. The bottleneck is now a strong hypothesis, not tooling.

2_parked_branch_lessons.donchian_s23_s24_s25: Profitable curve hid a weak entry: ENTRY_EDGE_NOT_SUPPORTED (S24); net flips negative removing the top 3 trades; realized drawdown only ~11th percentile of shuffles (path luck); OOS only 16 trades. A profitable equity curve is NOT an edge.
2_parked_branch_lessons.s26_trend_sr_ema_rsi: OOS PASS is not enough. Thin ~0.06R IS edge, FRICTION_SENSITIVE; entry significance INCONCLUSIVE (entry never distinguished from the trend filter); regime INCONCLUSIVE and inconsistent across NQ/ES; whole record lives in the post-2016 trend with a shared 2014-2016 collapse; OOS single-year-dominated (NQ 2024 61.7%, ES 2025 72.8%). PARK_CANDIDATE.
2_parked_branch_lessons.s27_mean_reversion_bull: IS_FAIL. Net -1.75R, only 18 trades (<25 floor), PF 0.84, expectancy -0.097R, 3/10 positive years, top-3 removal -6.25R. Two-step confirmation over-restricted the sample. IS_FAIL must stop before OOS.
2_parked_branch_lessons.s28_breakout_retest: IS_FAIL. Net -0.72R, only 12 trades in 10 years, PF 0.91, expectancy -0.06R, 3/10 positive years, top-3 removal -6.72R. A 5-filter stack starved the trade count. Raw breakout entry plus heavy filtering = clean negative.
what_s29_must_avoid: weak / inconclusive entry edge not distinct from a trend filter (Donchian, S26)
top-winner / fat-tail dependence (net dies without top 3)
low trade count from over-stacked confirmations (S27 18, S28 12)
thin IS expectancy with no friction headroom (S26 ~0.06R)
friction sensitivity (full-history edge negative under realistic cost)

3_candidate_comparison.name: Bad-regime avoidance / no-trade classifier (standalone overlay)
3_candidate_comparison.hypothesis: A mechanical classifier can flag low-expectancy 'do-not-trade' regimes (e.g. 2014-2016-style chop) and improve any strategy by sitting out.
3_candidate_comparison.why_edge_may_exist: Volatility/trend state is autocorrelated; bad regimes are partly identifiable in advance.
3_candidate_comparison.avoids_prior_failures: Directly encodes the S26 chop-collapse lesson; but it is a FILTER, not an entry.
3_candidate_comparison.expected_trade_count: ZERO standalone -- it generates no entries of its own.
3_candidate_comparison.regime_risk: It IS a regime model, but contributes no standalone P&L.
3_candidate_comparison.multimarket_testability: Testable as an overlay, not as a strategy.
3_candidate_comparison.first_clean_test: Would require pairing with an existing entry -- i.e. an overlay on a parked branch.
3_candidate_comparison.biggest_failure_mode: No entries => cannot be evaluated as a branch; only meaningful bolted onto another strategy (would be a tweak of a parked branch).
3_candidate_comparison.entry_significance_testable: NO -- there is no entry to test.

3_candidate_comparison.name: Volatility compression -> expansion after multi-day range contraction
3_candidate_comparison.hypothesis: Multi-day volatility contraction (e.g. NR7 / inside-day / narrow ATR) precedes a directional expansion; enter on the break of the contracted range.
3_candidate_comparison.why_edge_may_exist: Volatility clustering is one of the most robust empirical regularities -- compression reliably precedes expansion.
3_candidate_comparison.avoids_prior_failures: Edge is in the volatility STATE (regime-agnostic; compression happens in trend AND chop), so less single-regime-dependent than S26.
3_candidate_comparison.expected_trade_count: Moderate-to-good (compression setups recur several times per year).
3_candidate_comparison.friction_sensitivity: Decent headroom -- expansion moves can be large.
3_candidate_comparison.regime_risk: Lower regime concentration than S26; compression occurs across regimes.
3_candidate_comparison.multimarket_testability: High -- NQ and ES independently on daily OHLC.
3_candidate_comparison.first_clean_test: IS baseline of range-break entries conditioned on a pre-registered compression trigger; entry significance vs random days.
3_candidate_comparison.biggest_failure_mode: Compression predicts MAGNITUDE, not DIRECTION; a directional break entry re-inherits the unproven raw-breakout edge -> entry significance may come back INCONCLUSIVE on direction (the S26 trap).
3_candidate_comparison.entry_significance_testable: YES, but the directional component is the weak link.

3_candidate_comparison.name: Failed-breakout (false-break) reversal
3_candidate_comparison.hypothesis: A breakout of an N-day extreme that FAILS (price closes back inside the prior range) predicts a reversal away from the false break; fade it.
3_candidate_comparison.why_edge_may_exist: Structural / behavioral: a false break traps breakout entrants who are then forced to cover, mechanically pushing price back -- a DIRECTIONAL edge with a causal mechanism, not just autocorrelation.
3_candidate_comparison.avoids_prior_failures: It is the INVERSE of the two breakout failures (Donchian, S28); counter-trend reversal, so it does not ride a trend filter (unlike S26/S27) and is not post-2016-trend-dependent -- it may even profit in the 2014-2016 chop where S26 collapsed.
3_candidate_comparison.expected_trade_count: Moderate -- false breaks recur regularly; keep ONE trigger to preserve >=40 IS trades.
3_candidate_comparison.friction_sensitivity: Reversals can be sharp -> reasonable headroom; must still be verified full-history under >=0.10R.
3_candidate_comparison.regime_risk: Likely CONCENTRATED in choppy/range regimes (the OPPOSITE of S26) -- a complement, and the regime module will be informative; must check it survives trend regimes.
3_candidate_comparison.multimarket_testability: High -- NQ primary, ES independent, daily OHLC.
3_candidate_comparison.first_clean_test: IS baseline of false-break fades vs (a) a random-day null AND (b) a raw-breakout-day null, to prove the FAILURE carries the edge.
3_candidate_comparison.biggest_failure_mode: Definitional parameter risk ('what counts as failed'); regime-concentration in chop with losses in strong trends.
3_candidate_comparison.entry_significance_testable: YES, cleanly -- false-break forward returns vs random-day and vs raw-breakout nulls; direction is predicted.

3_candidate_comparison.name: Trend-day continuation after strong close + next-day confirmation
3_candidate_comparison.hypothesis: A strong-close trend day predicts next-day continuation; enter on confirmation.
3_candidate_comparison.avoids_prior_failures: Weakly -- it is close to S26 trend-following and risks the same failures.
3_candidate_comparison.friction_sensitivity: HIGH -- continuation moves are small; thin edge, friction-sensitive (the S26 weakness).
3_candidate_comparison.regime_risk: HIGH -- strongly trend-regime-dependent; 2014-2016 chop collapse risk.
3_candidate_comparison.first_clean_test: IS baseline of confirmed continuation entries; entry significance vs random.
3_candidate_comparison.biggest_failure_mode: Entry edge hard to distinguish from the trend itself (S26 redux); thin and friction-sensitive.
3_candidate_comparison.entry_significance_testable: Partially -- hard to separate from trend exposure.

3_candidate_comparison.name: Pullback-to-VWAP/EMA proxy with breadth-style confirmation (NQ internal)
3_candidate_comparison.hypothesis: Pullbacks to a moving-average mean in an uptrend resume; breadth confirms.
3_candidate_comparison.avoids_prior_failures: Poorly -- this is essentially S26 (EMA pullback) and S27 (mean reversion in bull trend), both PARKED; risks being a tweak of a parked branch.
3_candidate_comparison.friction_sensitivity: Likely thin (S26 showed pullback entries INCONCLUSIVE).
3_candidate_comparison.data_requirement: Needs breadth/internal data NQ may not have on daily OHLC -> data-requirement risk.
3_candidate_comparison.first_clean_test: IS baseline; but S26 already returned INCONCLUSIVE on the same family.
3_candidate_comparison.biggest_failure_mode: Re-tests an already-parked family; breadth data may be unavailable.
3_candidate_comparison.entry_significance_testable: Already effectively tested in S26 -> INCONCLUSIVE.

3_candidate_comparison.name: Daily opening-gap fade (gap relative to prior close)
3_candidate_comparison.hypothesis: Large daily opening gaps partially fill; fade the gap toward the prior close.
3_candidate_comparison.why_edge_may_exist: Overreaction / mean reversion of overnight gaps.
3_candidate_comparison.avoids_prior_failures: Distinct from all parked branches; counter-trend, not trend-dependent.
3_candidate_comparison.friction_sensitivity: HIGH -- gap-fade targets are small; thin edge.
3_candidate_comparison.regime_risk: Moderate; gap behavior shifts with volatility regime.
3_candidate_comparison.multimarket_testability: Testable on daily OHLC, but daily bars under-represent the true open/overnight dynamics (better on intraday) -> measurement risk.
3_candidate_comparison.data_requirement: Daily OHLC (imperfect for an overnight-driven effect).
3_candidate_comparison.first_clean_test: IS baseline of gap fades vs random; horizon = same-day close.
3_candidate_comparison.biggest_failure_mode: Thin per-trade edge + daily bars are a poor instrument for an intraday/overnight effect.
3_candidate_comparison.entry_significance_testable: YES, but small effect size and instrument mismatch.

4_ranking_best_to_worst: 1. Idea 3 -- Failed-breakout (false-break) reversal: directional structural edge, anti-trend-concentration, daily-testable on NQ/ES, distinct from every parked branch, entry significance cleanly testable.
2. Idea 2 -- Volatility compression -> expansion: robust vol-clustering basis and lower regime concentration, but the directional break entry re-inherits the unproven raw-breakout edge.
3. Idea 4 -- Trend-day continuation: high trade count but thin, friction-sensitive, trend-regime-dependent (S26 redux).
4. Idea 1 -- Bad-regime no-trade classifier: conceptually valuable but NOT a standalone branch (no entries, entry significance untestable); better repurposed later as an overlay/factory module.
5. Idea 6 -- Opening-gap fade: distinct but thin and poorly served by daily bars (instrument mismatch).
6. Idea 5 -- Pullback-to-VWAP/EMA + breadth: too close to the already-parked S26/S27 family and needs breadth data NQ may lack.

5_selected_hypothesis.name: Failed-breakout (false-break) reversal on daily NQ/ES
5_selected_hypothesis.statement: When price breaks an N-day extreme intrabar but closes back inside the prior range (a failed breakout), fade the break: enter in the reversal direction with an R-based stop beyond the false-break extreme and a fixed-R / mean-revert target.

6_why_it_won: Strongest, most DISTINCT entry edge with a causal mechanism (trapped breakout traders forced to cover) -- it predicts DIRECTION, not just magnitude, which is exactly what every parked branch lacked.

Most directly avoids the documented failure modes: it is the inverse of the Donchian/S28 raw-breakout failures and is counter-trend, so it does not ride a trend filter (S26/S27) and is not post-2016-trend-dependent -- it may even profit in the 2014-2016 chop that sank S26.
Entry significance is cleanly testable against BOTH a random-day null and a raw-breakout null, so the factory can prove the FAILURE (not the breakout) carries the edge.
Daily-data testable on NQ primary and ES independently with existing data; no intraday dependency.
Not a tweak of any parked branch -- no parked branch was a reversal/fade.

7_why_the_others_lost.idea_2_vol_compression: Strong second, but compression predicts magnitude not direction; the directional break entry re-inherits the unproven raw-breakout edge and risks an INCONCLUSIVE entry-significance result like S26.
7_why_the_others_lost.idea_4_trend_continuation: Too close to S26 trend-following; thin, friction-sensitive, trend-regime-concentrated -> repeats the S26 failure.
7_why_the_others_lost.idea_1_no_trade_classifier: Generates no entries standalone; cannot meet 'strongest entry edge' or 'entry significance testable'; only useful bolted onto a parked branch -> a tweak, not a new branch.
7_why_the_others_lost.idea_6_gap_fade: Thin per-trade edge and daily bars are a poor instrument for an overnight/intraday effect.
7_why_the_others_lost.idea_5_pullback_breadth: Essentially the already-parked S26/S27 mean-reversion-in-trend family (S26 entry significance was INCONCLUSIVE) and needs breadth data NQ may lack.

8_key_risks: Regime concentration in choppy/range regimes with losses in strong trends -- must be checked by the regime + walk-forward modules (expected to be the OPPOSITE concentration to S26).
Definitional parameter risk: 'what counts as a failed break' (N, close-back threshold) must be FROZEN in S29-D2 before any run; no post-hoc tuning.
Trade-count risk: do NOT stack multiple confirmation filters (the S27/S28 trade-count killer); keep ONE trigger + stop + target, target >=40 IS trades.
Friction: a reversal edge must stay positive full-history under >=0.10R/trade, not just OOS.
Top-winner dependence: net must stay positive without the top 3 trades (the Donchian/S25 gate).
Year/OOS concentration: profit must spread across years, not sit in one (the S26 gate).

9_proposed_s29_d2_spec_direction.core_trigger: Single frozen trigger: an N-day (e.g. 20-day) extreme is exceeded intrabar but the bar closes back inside the prior range -> fade in the reversal direction. Symmetric for failed upside and failed downside breaks.
9_proposed_s29_d2_spec_direction.risk_model: R-based: stop just beyond the false-break extreme; target a fixed R (and/or revert-to-mean such as EMA20 / prior swing). R-only accounting, one position at a time.
9_proposed_s29_d2_spec_direction.anti_overfit_pre_registration: Freeze N, the close-back-inside definition, stop and target BEFORE any run. No parameter sweeps; any change opens a new branch.
9_proposed_s29_d2_spec_direction.pre_registered_is_gates: Trade-count floor >=40 (FAIL <25); PF >=1.30; expectancy >0; positive years >=6/10; net positive WITHOUT top 3 winners; IS max DD <= IS net.
9_proposed_s29_d2_spec_direction.entry_significance_plan: Test false-break forward returns vs (a) a random-day null and (b) a raw-breakout-day null; require EDGE_LIKELY, not merely beating random.
9_proposed_s29_d2_spec_direction.regime_and_walk_forward_plan: Explicitly classify trend vs chop; EXPECT chop concentration and test trend-regime survival; surface 2014-2016 behavior specifically (the inverse test of S26).
9_proposed_s29_d2_spec_direction.friction_plan: Require the FULL-HISTORY edge positive under a conservative per-trade R-cost (>=0.10R), not just OOS.
9_proposed_s29_d2_spec_direction.multimarket_plan: NQ primary; ES as the one genuinely independent confirmation; micros descriptive only.
9_proposed_s29_d2_spec_direction.discipline: IS first under the sealed 2013-2022 window; pre-register the OOS protocol and COMMIT it before touching 2023-2025; one-shot OOS; no tuning after OOS.

10_final_line: S29-D1 is idea sourcing only; no strategy code, backtest, OOS, optimization, paper trading, or live trading is authorized.
trading_recommendation: NONE. Idea-sourcing memo only. Donchian, S26, S27, S28 remain PARKED; no active strategy; no paper/live system exists or is authorized. The selected S29 hypothesis (failed-breakout reversal) is a research direction, not a trade.
